object = <function accuracy at 0x7fd994405ea0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def test_mean_average_precision(target_framework, mode, backend_compile):
        trace_args = (
            [torch.tensor([[100, 50, 150, 100.]])],
            [torch.tensor([1.])],
            [torch.tensor([0.7])],
            [torch.tensor([[100, 50, 150, 100.]])],
            [torch.tensor([1.])],
            2,
        )
        trace_kwargs = {}
        test_args = (
            [torch.tensor([[50, 25, 75, 50], [100, 50, 150, 100.]])],
            [torch.tensor([1, 2.])],
            [torch.tensor([0.6, 0.8])],
            [torch.tensor([[50, 25, 75, 50], [100, 50, 150, 100.]])],
            [torch.tensor([1, 2.])],
            3,
        )
        kornia.metrics.mean_average_precision(*trace_args)
        kornia.metrics.mean_average_precision(*test_args)
        test_kwargs = {}
    
        # NOTE: this test fails due to the use of dynamic control flow; skipping

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function mean_iou_bbox at 0x7fd9944069e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.

                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function ssim3d at 0x7fd994407130>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function warp_perspective at 0x7f81c8c23910>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function warp_affine3d at 0x7f81c8c381f0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function warp_grid at 0x7f81c8c23ac0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function rotate at 0x7f81c8c38ca0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function shear at 0x7f81c8c39000>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function pyrdown at 0x7f81c8c3b6d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function build_laplacian_pyramid at 0x7f81c8c3beb0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function upscale_double at 0x7f81c8c3bf40>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function get_perspective_transform3d at 0x7f81c8c383a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function get_shear_matrix3d at 0x7f81c8c380d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function projection_from_Rt at 0x7f81c8c38280>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function crop_by_boxes at 0x7f81c8c39ea0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.transform.affwarp.Rescale'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function add_weighted at 0x7fc245ea60e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function adjust_gamma at 0x7fc245ea45e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well

object = <function adjust_log at 0x7fc245ea4940>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function equalize3d at 0x7fc245ea5090>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.

                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function image_histogram2d at 0x7fc245ea6b00>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function zca_mean at 0x7fc245ec1090>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function jpeg_codec_differentiable at 0x7fc245ea7f40>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def transpile(

        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",

    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function solve_quadratic at 0x7f30f2e005e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function determinant_to_polynomial at 0x7f30f2e00820>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function essential_from_Rt at 0x7fd283211990>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function relative_camera_motion at 0x7fd283211b40>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function fundamental_from_projections at 0x7fd283212440>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function normalize_transformation at 0x7fd283211fc0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246:

object = <function symmetrical_epipolar_distance at 0x7fd2832100d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function projection_from_KRt at 0x7fd2832112d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function random_intrinsics at 0x7fd2832111b0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function draw_line at 0x7f9014e5f6d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function create_meshgrid3d at 0x7f9014e5fb50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

kornia/geometry/test_boxes.py:34: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.boxes.Boxes'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

        The translated object.

object = <class 'kornia.x.callbacks.EarlyStopping'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

target_framework = 'tensorflow', mode = 's2s', backend_compile = False

    def test_Swin2SR(target_framework, mode, backend_compile):
        # NOTE: this is in the form `integration.subsection.model`, the class submodule would just be transformers.Swin2SRModel
        print("transformers.vision.Swin2SRModel")
    
        dataset = load_dataset("huggingface/cats-image", trust_remote_code=True)
        image = dataset["test"]["image"][0]
        image_processor = AutoImageProcessor.from_pretrained("caidas/swin2SR-classical-sr-x2-64")
    
        # modify the config to avoid OOM issues
        swin2sr_config = Swin2SRConfig()
        swin2sr_config.embed_dim = 2
        swin2sr_config.depths = [2, 2]
        swin2sr_config.num_heads = [2, 2]
    
        torch_model = Swin2SRModel.from_pretrained("caidas/swin2SR-classical-sr-x2-64", config=swin2sr_config, ignore_mismatched_sizes=True)
        torch_inputs = image_processor(image, return_tensors="pt")
        with torch.no_grad():
            torch_outputs = torch_model(**torch_inputs)
        torch_last_hidden_states = torch_outputs.last_hidden_state

object = <class 'kornia.tracking.planar_tracker.HomographyTracker'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function pol2cart at 0x7fb74e27a290>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function convert_points_to_homogeneous at 0x7fb74e27a440>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function denormalize_pixel_coordinates3d at 0x7fb74e27b2e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source
