        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well

object = <function quaternion_to_axis_angle at 0x7fb74e27ab90>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function normalize_quaternion at 0x7fb74e27aa70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function axis_angle_to_quaternion at 0x7fb74e27ae60>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function Rt_to_matrix4x4 at 0x7fb74e27b7f0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function camtoworld_to_worldtocam_Rt at 0x7fb74e27bb50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function camtoworld_vision_to_graphics_Rt at 0x7fb74e27bac0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.nerf.nerf_model.MLP'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_container.py:289: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation.container.patch.PatchSequential'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/test_feature3.py:125: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.feature.responses.BlobDoGSingle'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function load_image at 0x7f311833e560>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function distort_points at 0x7fa7fdd940d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function conv_soft_argmax2d at 0x7f54c702a0e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function spatial_softmax2d at 0x7f54c7029360>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function nms2d at 0x7f54c7029900>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.subpix.spatial_soft_argmax.ConvQuadInterp3d'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function find_homography_dlt_iterated at 0x7fcf864baef0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function line_segment_transfer_error_one_way at 0x7fcf864bad40>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------

object = <function oneway_transfer_error at 0x7fcf864bab90>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.feature.orientation.LAFOrienter'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function bbox_generator at 0x7f5a80757b50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246:

object = <function bbox_to_mask3d at 0x7f5a80757ac0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function transform_bbox at 0x7f5a80757c70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function box_blur at 0x7f093bd05360>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function joint_bilateral_blur at 0x7f093bd04ee0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function unsharp_mask at 0x7f093b7b3910>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function sobel at 0x7f093bd06dd0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function filter2d at 0x7f093bd055a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246:

object = <function get_gaussian_erf_kernel1d at 0x7f093bd04160>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.

            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.

object = <function compute_padding at 0x7ff0d263d750>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function distance_transform at 0x7ff0d263cee0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.contrib.extract_patches.CombineTensorPatches'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function erosion at 0x7fd802733f40>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function gradient at 0x7fd802754160>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function gftt_response at 0x7f82ca2a3b50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function dog_response at 0x7f82ca2a3c70>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function match_nn at 0x7f82ca100700>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function match_fginn at 0x7f82ca100940>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function extract_patches_simple at 0x7f82ca257e20>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function laf_to_boundary_points at 0x7f82ca257b50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function rgb_to_grayscale at 0x7efe2bc0f370>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function bgr_to_rgb at 0x7efe2bc0ef80>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function bgr_to_rgba at 0x7efe2bc0f0a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function rgb_to_hls at 0x7efe2bc0fa30>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function hsv_to_rgb at 0x7efe2b61c160>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source
