        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function rgb_to_yuv at 0x7efe2b61d750>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function yuv420_to_rgb at 0x7efe2b61d990>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function xyz_to_rgb at 0x7efe2b61c5e0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.color.gray.GrayscaleToRgb'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/test_color.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.color.hls.HlsToRgb'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.color.lab.LabToRgb'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.color.yuv.Yuv420ToRgb'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.

                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

object = <class 'kornia.geometry.liegroup.so3.So3'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function get_laf_scale at 0x7f61c84d3760>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function set_laf_orientation at 0x7f61c84d39a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function laf_to_three_points at 0x7f61c84f8160>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.feature.mkd.MKDDescriptor'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.auto_contrast.RandomAutoContrast'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.contrast.RandomContrast'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.gaussian_noise.RandomGaussianNoise'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.linear_illumination.RandomLinearIllumination'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.intensity.plasma.RandomPlasmaContrast'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.geometric.pad.PadTo'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

kornia/augmentation/test_augmentation.py:31: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._2d.geometric.thin_plate_spline.RandomThinPlateSpline'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <class 'kornia.augmentation._3d.geometric.horizontal_flip.RandomHorizontalFlip3D'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <function relative_transformation at 0x7f117c2b7520>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function inverse_transformation at 0x7f117c2b7490>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """

                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

object = <function ssim3d_loss at 0x7f627a1611b0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function inverse_depth_smoothness_loss at 0x7f627a13d6c0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.

                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function cauchy_loss at 0x7f627a13c3a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function lovasz_softmax_loss at 0x7f627a13f0a0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <class 'kornia.losses.hausdorff.HausdorffERLoss3D'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.losses.cauchy.CauchyLoss'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.feature.integrated.SIFTFeature'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

object = <class 'kornia.feature.integrated.GFTTAffNetHardNet'>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def transpile(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import transpile as _transpile

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function project_points_z1 at 0x7f26e1535c60>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function unproject_points_orthographic at 0x7f26e1535ab0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function dx_distort_points_affine at 0x7f26e1b57760>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246: 
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

object = <function dx_distort_points_kannala_brandt at 0x7f26e1b57b50>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

object = <function unproject_points at 0x7f26e1b57e20>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

helpers.py:246:

object = <function depth_from_disparity at 0x7ff1286df6d0>, source = 'torch', target = 'tensorflow', reuse_existing = True

    def source_to_source(
        object,
        source: str = "torch",
        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source

        target: str = "tensorflow",
        reuse_existing: bool = True,
    ):
        """Converts a given object (class/function) from one framework to another.
    
        This function performs source-to-source translation of a given object from the source framework
        to the target framework.
    
        The object can be translated between two frameworks or between the Ivy IR as well
        e.g. (source="torch_frontend", target="ivy") or (source="torch_frontend", target="tensorflow") etc.
    
        Args:
        ----
            object: The object (class/function) to be translated.
            source (str, optional): The source framework. Defaults to 'torch'.
            target (str, optional): The target framework. Defaults to 'tensorflow'.
            reuse_existing (bool, optional): If True, the function will check if `object`
                                             already exists in the translated directory and reuse it.
                                             If False, it will re-translate `object`,
                                             even if it already exists in the directory, and overwrite
                                             the old implementation. Defaults to 'True'.
    
        Returns:
        -------
        The translated object.
        """
>       from ._compiler import source_to_source as _source_to_source
